introSection: Raven is a wooden roller coaster located at Holiday World & Splashin' Safari in Santa Claus, Indiana, USA. It has repeatedly won the Best Wooden Coaster Golden Ticket Award. The ride opened in 1995 and features a long underground tunnel.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Custom Coasters International, Inc. met with Holiday World to discuss a new roller coaster in June 1994 and visited the park to survey the site the following month. CCI then collaborated with Holiday World, and in particular Will Koch, to create Raven's design.

Groundbreaking for the Raven occurred in September 1994. Raven officially opened on May 6, 1995, following a media day on May 3. The ride's name was suggested by Amusement Business editor Tim O'Brien.

When the ride first opened, it featured just one train. A second train was installed in 2005 to coincide with its 10th anniversary. Space had been set aside for a future transfer table when the ride was originally designed. The ride's train also originally featured buzz bars.

The ride was designed an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark by American Coaster Enthusiasts on June 23, 2016 during Coaster Con XXXIX.

During the 2020-2021 off-season, Raven was given a refurbishment. The ride's trains were repainted with wings on both sides and 25% of the track was replaced.

New pre-cut track from The Gravity Group, LLC was added to the ride in 2025.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: The ride is named after Edgar Allan Poe's poem "The Raven".

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters. Riders are held in by individual ratcheting lap bars and seatbelts.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A 32-year-old woman fell to her death from the ride at approximately 8:30pm on May 31, 2003. The ride was temporarily closed after the incident and reopened on June 7.

fullText:
Raven is a wooden roller coaster located at Holiday World & Splashin' Safari in Santa Claus, Indiana, USA. It has repeatedly won the Best Wooden Coaster Golden Ticket Award. The ride opened in 1995 and features a long underground tunnel.

Custom Coasters International, Inc. met with Holiday World to discuss a new roller coaster in June 1994 and visited the park to survey the site the following month. CCI then collaborated with Holiday World, and in particular Will Koch, to create Raven's design.

Groundbreaking for the Raven occurred in September 1994. Raven officially opened on May 6, 1995, following a media day on May 3. The ride's name was suggested by Amusement Business editor Tim O'Brien.

When the ride first opened, it featured just one train. A second train was installed in 2005 to coincide with its 10th anniversary. Space had been set aside for a future transfer table when the ride was originally designed. The ride's train also originally featured buzz bars.

The ride was designed an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark by American Coaster Enthusiasts on June 23, 2016 during Coaster Con XXXIX.

During the 2020-2021 off-season, Raven was given a refurbishment. The ride's trains were repainted with wings on both sides and 25% of the track was replaced.

New pre-cut track from The Gravity Group, LLC was added to the ride in 2025.

The ride is named after Edgar Allan Poe's poem "The Raven".



The trains were built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters. Riders are held in by individual ratcheting lap bars and seatbelts.

A 32-year-old woman fell to her death from the ride at approximately 8:30pm on May 31, 2003. The ride was temporarily closed after the incident and reopened on June 7.
